highlighted_phrases_are_potential_red_: 🚩 Highlighted phrases are potential red flags (a heuristic, not a verdict):
welcome_to_the_ai_literacy_lab: Welcome to the AI Literacy Lab
this_is_a_six_section_lab_about_how_ai: This is a six-section lab about how AI actually works, where it fails, and how to use it without letting it do the thinking you need to practice.
six_sections_no_order_required_revisit: Six sections, no order required. Revisit anything. Your progress saves.
press_1_6_to_jump_sections_for_all_sho: Press 1–6 to jump sections, ? for all shortcuts, T for teacher notes.
glossary_definitions_pop_up_inline_the: Glossary definitions pop up inline. The full glossary is one button away in the banner.
the_live_demos_here_use_google_gemini_: The live demos here use Google Gemini. Don't paste names, phone numbers, or anything private — prompts may be logged by the AI provider.
see_tokens_next_token_prediction_and_t: See tokens, next-token prediction, and temperature in action.
a_gallery_of_real_feeling_ai_mistakes_: A gallery of real-feeling AI mistakes, with why each one fools people.
weak_vs_strong_prompts_five_patterns_a: Weak vs. strong prompts, five patterns, and live iteration.
12_phrases_identify_the_role_of_each_p: 12 phrases. Identify the role of each (persona / task / context / format / example / constraint).
click_the_wrong_parts_of_an_ai_generat: Click the wrong parts of an AI-generated passage.
scaffold_vs_substitute_a_udl_framed_de: Scaffold vs. substitute — a UDL-framed decision tool. Bring your own situation.
one_page_printable_summary_of_the_whol: One-page printable summary of the whole lab.
this_clears_which_sections_you_ve_visi: This clears which sections you’ve visited, your saved prompt iterations, and your UDL reflections. XP you earned elsewhere in AlloFlow is not affected.
the_ai_in_alloflow_is_not_magic_it_is_: The AI in AlloFlow is not magic — it is a next-token predictor trained on a huge pile of text. This lab shows what it is really doing, when it fails, and how to use it without letting it do the thinking you need to practice.
see_all_glossary_terms_in_one_place: See all glossary terms in one place
clear_your_progress_and_start_fresh: Clear your progress and start fresh
live_ai_calls_are_not_available_in_thi: Live AI calls are not available in this environment. Demos will use recorded examples.
type_something_into_the_tokenizer_in_s: Type something into the tokenizer in Section 1.
compare_outputs_at_different_temperatu: Compare outputs at different temperatures in Section 1.
write_or_run_3_prompts_in_the_section_: Write or run 3 prompts in the Section 3 workshop.
catch_every_error_in_a_section_4_passa: Catch every error in a Section 4 passage without hints.
work_through_all_7_preset_scenarios_in: Work through all 7 preset scenarios in Section 5.
pass_all_5_comprehension_checks_one_pe: Pass all 5 comprehension checks (one per content section).
visit_every_section_including_the_quic: Visit every section, including the Quick Reference.
stuff_you_may_have_picked_up_that_is_n: — stuff you may have picked up that is not quite right
about_the_ai_in_this_lab: About the AI in this lab
powers_live_demos_here_it_is: powers live demos here. It is
llm_among_many_chatgpt_claude_and_othe: LLM among many — ChatGPT, Claude, and others exist, and each behaves differently on the same prompt.
they_don_t_know_events_after_it_ask_th: — they don't know events after it. Ask the AI directly what its cutoff is if you need to know for time-sensitive questions.
what_you_type_may_be_logged_by_the_ai_: What you type may be logged by the AI provider.
names_phone_numbers_medical_info_or_an: names, phone numbers, medical info, or anything you wouldn't write on a note that a stranger could read.
this_lab_is_about_text_ai_the_same_ide: This lab is about TEXT AI. The same ideas apply to...
they_hallucinate_hands_text_and_anatom: They hallucinate hands, text, and anatomy the same way text models hallucinate facts. Bias in training data shows up in what they draw.
can_clone_voices_from_a_few_seconds_of: Can clone voices from a few seconds of recording. Consent and deepfake risks apply.
newer_and_even_more_convincing_than_de: Newer and even more convincing than deepfakes used to be. Assume any short clip could be synthetic.
the_core_literacy_skills_check_sources: The core literacy skills — check sources, notice when tone doesn't match certainty, treat output as draft not truth — transfer across all of these.
written_by_a_clinician_psyd_with_stude: Written by a clinician (PsyD) with students who have executive-function, attention, and learning differences in mind. The rubric in Section 5 is the part most AI-literacy curricula miss — it is also the most important.
your_prompt_or_the_model_s_own_previou: Your prompt or the model's own previous output.
split_text_into_small_pieces_tokens_wo: Split text into small pieces (tokens). Words, sub-words, punctuation.
assign_a_probability_to_every_possible: Assign a probability to every possible next token.
temperature_picks_how_random_the_choic: Temperature picks how random the choice is (see the playground below).
add_the_new_token_feed_it_back_in_do_t: Add the new token. Feed it back in. Do this hundreds of times.
everything_an_llm_does_is_a_loop_of_th: Everything an LLM does is a loop of these 5 steps. Not "thinking" — pattern matching on tokens, over and over.
the_newly_sampled_token_becomes_part_o: The newly sampled token becomes part of the input for the next step. One sentence of AI output is hundreds of these loops.
how_far_from_the_top_pick: How far from the top pick
the_full_pipeline_at_a_glance: 🛤 The full pipeline at a glance
each_block_below_explores_one_of_these: Each block below explores one of these stages. Click any chip to jump straight to it.
an_llm_never_sees_words_it_sees: An LLM never sees "words" — it sees
which_are_pieces_of_words_type_a_sente: , which are pieces of words. Type a sentence and watch it split.
the_dog_was_running_quickly_because_it: The dog was running quickly because it was unhappy.
i_had_pizza_and_ice_cream_at_the_park_: I had pizza 🍕 and ice cream 🍨 at the park 🏞️ today!
the_paleontologist_misidentified_the_p: The paleontologist misidentified the pseudoscorpion as an arthropod.
the_model_predicts_the_next_token_not_: The model predicts the next TOKEN, not the next word. Short common words become one token; longer words often split (e.g. "unhappy" → "un" + "happy"). This is also how billing works — API costs are per token, not per word.
all_the_llm_is_really_doing_guessing_w: All the LLM is really doing: guessing what token comes next. Given the same context, it has a PROBABILITY for every possible next token.
controls_how_random_the_next_token_cho: controls how RANDOM the next-token choice is. Low (0.2) = pick the highest-probability token almost always. High (1.2) = sample from the long tail, including unlikely tokens. Same prompt, three temperatures:
show_the_recorded_example_outputs_agai: Show the recorded example outputs again
low_temperature_is_right_for_facts_wha: Low temperature is right for facts ("what is the capital of France?"). High temperature is right for creative work. Most AI chat tools default somewhere in the middle — which is why a factual question sometimes drifts.
pick_any_temperature_and_any_prompt_ru: Pick any temperature and any prompt. Run it live. Then run the same prompt at a different temperature and compare. The last 5 runs stay visible so you can watch the output shift with the dial.
live_ai_required_for_the_playground_th: Live AI required for the playground — the 3-preset compare above uses recorded examples.
llms_don_t_know_what_they_don_t_know_t: LLMs don't know what they don't know. They generate the most plausible-sounding next token even when the plausible answer is wrong — this is called
here_are_seven_failure_modes_every_stu: ). Here are seven failure modes every student should recognize.
the_date_its_training_data_ends_anythi: — the date its training data ends. Anything after that is unknown, though the model may still sound confident. Ask it:
if_the_question_is_about_something_tha: If the question is about something that could have changed or happened recently, do not trust the AI's answer without a live web source. You can also ask the model directly what its cutoff is — it usually knows.
the_llm_doesn_t_have_a_truth_step_it_h: The LLM doesn't have a "truth" step. It has a "plausible" step. The exact same pipeline produces both right and wrong answers — with the same confident tone.
picks_likely_next_tokens_has_no_fact_c: Picks likely next tokens. Has no fact check.
the_pattern_was_common_in_training_dat: The pattern was common in training data, so the model matched it (not a lookup — still prediction).
fact_wasn_t_present_or_the_pattern_loo: Fact wasn't present, or the pattern looks right but isn't.
both_paths_come_out_the_same_way_same_: both paths come out the same way — same confident tone, same fluent sentences. The only way to tell them apart is to check against something outside the AI.
same_prompt_three_different_models_rea: Same prompt, three different models. Read carefully — the science is the same, but the voice, length, and hedging differ. If your class tested with one model and your friend tested with another, you’d get different answers and both would be "right."
length_varies_by_10_words_one_model_le: Length varies by 10+ words. One model leads with the explanation, another with the visible effect. One uses "wavelengths" explicitly, another says "colors." None is more correct — but if you needed a specific phrasing for an assignment, the model you picked would matter.
recorded_examples_for_teaching_not_liv: Recorded examples for teaching, not live calls. Live calls here use Gemini only.
send_this_exact_question_to_gemini_rig: Send this exact question to Gemini right now and see what comes back
compare_the_live_answer_to_the_canned_: Compare the live answer to the canned one above.
if_ai_confidence_tracked_correctness_t: If AI-confidence tracked correctness, the dots below would form a diagonal. Instead, almost every AI answer reads as high-confidence — whether it's right or not. The tone is the same.
scatter_plot_ai_confidence_on_the_x_ax: Scatter plot: AI confidence on the x axis vs. accuracy on the y axis. Most answers cluster at high confidence regardless of accuracy.
if_ai_confidence_tracked_truth_dots_wo: If AI confidence tracked truth, dots would line up on the dashed diagonal.
in_the_bottom_right_are_the_danger_zon: in the bottom-right are the danger zone: sounds sure, actually wrong.
look_identical_to_red_in_tone_that_s_t: look identical to red in tone. That's the teaching point.
you_can_t_tell_which_is_which_from_ins: You can't tell which is which from inside the AI. Only external checking works.
paste_any_ai_generated_text_from_here_: Paste any AI-generated text (from here, another chatbot, an article that claims to be AI-written, etc.). The tool highlights
kinds_of_red_flags_so_you_can_see_wher: kinds of red flags so you can see where to verify. No AI call required — this runs locally.
paste_an_ai_response_here_to_see_which: Paste an AI response here to see which phrases deserve a second look...
paste_ai_output_for_red_flag_analysis: Paste AI output for red-flag analysis
nothing_to_analyze_yet_paste_some_ai_g: Nothing to analyze yet. Paste some AI-generated text above to see which phrases might need verification.
this_removes_all_saved_prompts_from_th: This removes all saved prompts from this section. You can't undo it.
try_adding_i_am_a_9th_grader_studying_: Try adding: "I am a 9th grader studying for..." — tells the AI about YOU.
try_adding_a_don_t_don_t_write_the_who: Try adding a DON'T: "Don't write the whole essay for me." Constraints prevent unhelpful outputs.
try_adding_a_format_give_me_a_3_step_l: Try adding a format: "Give me a 3-step list" or "One sentence only" — controls how easy the answer is to USE.
try_adding_a_sample_here_s_one_i_did_e: Try adding a sample: "Here's one I did: [example]. Do the next one in the same style."
a_weak_prompt_gives_you_a_shallow_answ: A weak prompt gives you a shallow answer, fast. A strong prompt gives you a useful answer, still fast. The difference is craft — and it is very learnable.
here_is_one_strong_prompt_with_every_p: Here is one strong prompt with every pattern color-coded. Hover any phrase to see which pattern it is.
copy_to_use_in_your_own_ai_tool: Copy to use in your own AI tool
pick_a_task_fill_in_the_blanks_you_ll_: Pick a task. Fill in the blanks. You’ll end up with a prompt that uses all five patterns — without having to remember them.
load_into_the_workshop_below_to_run_it: Load into the workshop below to run it live or keep iterating
load_this_prompt_into_the_workshop_bel: Load this prompt into the workshop below so you can run it with Gemini
copy_this_prompt_to_try_in_your_own_ai: Copy this prompt to try in your own AI tool
run_both_prompts_at_the_same_time_and_: Run both prompts at the same time and compare what Gemini produces
your_turn_iterate_a_prompt_live: Your turn: iterate a prompt live
write_a_prompt_below_run_it_look_at_th: Write a prompt below. Run it. Look at the output. Edit the prompt using the five patterns. Run again. You're looking for the output to improve — or for you to understand WHY the output is shaped the way it is.
try_explain_why_the_ocean_is_salty_to_: Try: "Explain why the ocean is salty to a curious 7th grader who already knows what an atom is. Use no more than 4 sentences."
check_which_of_the_five_patterns_your_: Check which of the five patterns your prompt uses (no AI call needed)
copy_your_prompt_to_use_elsewhere: Copy your prompt to use elsewhere
save_this_prompt_to_your_journal_for_l: Save this prompt to your journal for later
live_ai_unavailable_the_critique_still: Live AI unavailable — the critique still works.
this_critique_uses_keyword_heuristics_: This critique uses keyword heuristics, not AI. It can miss creative phrasings. Use it as a checklist prompt, not a grade.
nothing_saved_yet_every_successful_liv: Nothing saved yet. Every successful live run auto-saves here. You can also ⭐ Save a prompt without running it.
save_your_journal_as_a_markdown_file: Save your journal as a Markdown file
the_audience_is_parents_who_don_t_have: The audience is parents who don't have a science background.
output_as_3_bullet_points_max_20_words: Output as 3 bullet points, max 20 words each.
for_instance_photosynthesis_is_how_pla: For instance: "Photosynthesis is how plants eat sunlight."
do_not_use_the_words_simply_or_just: Do not use the words "simply" or "just."
pretend_you_are_an_admissions_officer_: Pretend you are an admissions officer at a small liberal-arts college.
use_a_warm_but_professional_tone: Use a warm but professional tone.
the_parent_emailed_yesterday_asking_wh: The parent emailed yesterday asking why their child is on a behavior watchlist.
example_output_your_essay_shows_strong: Example output: "Your essay shows strong voice but lacks structure. Try…"
write_a_follow_up_email_to_the_parent: Write a follow-up email to the parent.
you_will_see_12_phrases_pulled_from_re: You will see 12 phrases pulled from realistic prompts. For each, pick which of six anatomical roles the phrase plays — persona, task, context, format, example, or constraint. Tests whether you can *parse* a prompt as an artifact, not just write or read one.
reveal_one_error_using_any_hints_forfe: Reveal one error. Using any hints forfeits the perfect-score bonus — but helps if you're stuck.
hints_used_perfect_bonus_forfeited_on_: Hints used — perfect bonus forfeited on this attempt. Reset and retry without hints to earn it.
pick_a_topic_you_know_well_write_a_sho: Pick a topic you know well. Write a short AI-style paragraph on it, but wrap 2–3 wrong phrases in double brackets like
trade_passages_with_someone_else_whoev: . Trade passages with someone else — whoever catches all the bracketed errors wins.
e_g_mitosis_has_four_main_phases_proph: e.g. "Mitosis has four main phases: prophase, metaphase, [[telophase]], and anaphase. During metaphase, chromosomes line up [[along the nuclear membrane]] before separating."
good_trap_passages_have_2_3_seeded_err: Good trap passages have 2–3 seeded errors. More than that feels unfair; fewer is too easy. Aim for errors that sound plausible at first read.
the_barrier_you_described_sounds_mecha: The barrier you described sounds mechanical (slow typing, memory retrieval, formatting, organization). Using AI to remove that barrier is not the same as having AI do the skill itself. Go ahead — but keep the core thinking on you.
the_barrier_is_cognitive_and_the_skill: The barrier is cognitive, and the skill you named is exactly the kind of skill school is trying to build (thinking, composing, reasoning). Letting AI do this means you skip the practice. Consider: can AI COACH you through the skill instead of performing it for you? That flips it back to scaffold.
the_barrier_is_cognitive_but_the_skill: The barrier is cognitive, but the skill you described may not be the one being practiced here. Re-read the assignment: what specific skill is it really testing? Use AI to reduce barriers to that specific skill, not to replace it.
the_question_is_not_is_ai_good_or_bad_: The question is not "is AI good or bad" — it's "does AI REMOVE a barrier to the skill I'm practicing, or does it REPLACE the skill itself?"
before_you_use_ai_for_something_run_it: Before you use AI for something, run it through these two questions. The answers route to one of three outcomes.
what_specific_skill_is_this_task_meant: What specific skill is this task meant to build?
is_ai_removing_a_barrier_to_that_skill: Is AI removing a BARRIER to that skill, or doing the SKILL itself?
removes_barrier_go_ahead_keep_the_core: Removes barrier. Go ahead — keep the core thinking on you.
can_ai_coach_you_through_the_skill_ins: Can AI coach you through the skill instead of doing it? If yes — reframe as scaffold.
doing_the_skill_itself_skipping_ai_her: Doing the skill itself. Skipping AI here preserves the practice you need.
use_ai_in_a_way_that_helps_you_do_the_: Use AI in a way that helps you DO the work yourself.
use_ai_to_do_the_work_for_you: Use AI to do the work FOR you.
now_compare_both_worked_versions_there: . Now compare both worked versions — there's no "wrong" pick, only clearer thinking about what the skill actually is.
the_five_scenarios_above_are_common_bu: The five scenarios above are common, but the real question is ALWAYS about your specific moment. Describe yours, name the skill, and name the barrier. You'll get a direction — not an answer.
e_g_i_have_a_biology_lab_report_due_fr: e.g. "I have a biology lab report due Friday and I can’t focus long enough to write the intro."
2_the_skill_the_assignment_is_practici: 2. The skill the assignment is practicing
e_g_writing_a_scientific_intro_that_st: e.g. "writing a scientific intro that states the hypothesis"
name_the_skill_this_assignment_is_prac: Name the skill this assignment is practicing
3_what_kind_of_barrier_is_in_the_way: 3. What kind of barrier is in the way?
live_ai_unavailable_here_the_heuristic: Live AI unavailable here — the heuristic direction above uses only your answers, no AI call needed.
to_print_other_ui_hides_automatically: to print; other UI hides automatically.
download_a_markdown_report_of_your_pro: Download a Markdown report of your progress, checks passed, and saved prompts. Hand to a teacher.
predicts_the_next_token_by_probability: Predicts the next token by probability
controls_how_random_the_pick_is: controls how random the pick is
it_has_no_idea_what_s_true_only_what_s: It has no idea what's "true" — only what's plausible
it_sounds_confident_exactly_the_same_w: It sounds confident exactly the same whether right or wrong
does_the_answer_cite_anything_verify_e: Does the answer cite anything? Verify each citation.
is_it_past_the_model_s_cutoff_use_web_: Is it past the model's cutoff? Use web search if so.
any_math_redo_it_with_a_calculator: Any math? Redo it with a calculator.
sound_too_confident_ask_are_you_sure_w: Sound too confident? Ask "are you sure?" — watch if the answer changes.
ai_removes_a_barrier_so_you_can_do_the: AI removes a barrier so YOU can do the skill. (Quiz me. Organize my ideas. Ask me questions.)
ai_does_the_skill_for_you_write_my_ess: AI does the skill for you. (Write my essay. Solve my problem. Summarize instead of read.)
ask_what_skill_is_this_assignment_real: Ask: "What skill is this assignment really testing?" Then keep that on you.
i_am_a_grade_student_don_t_solve_this_: "I am a [grade] student. Don't solve this for me — ask me questions that help me find the answer."
quiz_me_on_topic_one_question_at_a_tim: "Quiz me on [topic] one question at a time. Wait for my answer before the next."
are_you_certain_this_event_actually_ha: "Are you certain this event actually happened? Cite a source."
explain_concept_using_only_middle_scho: "Explain [concept] using only middle-school vocabulary. No jargon."
alloflow_ai_literacy_lab_built_for_stu: AlloFlow · AI Literacy Lab · built for students who need AI as a scaffold, not a substitute.
back_to_ai_literacy_lab_home: Back to AI Literacy Lab home
pick_another_answer_to_try_again: Pick another answer to try again.
low_stakes_high_familiarity_high_sourc: Low stakes, high familiarity, high source quality. Spot-check still good practice, but you can trust the output.
trust_most_of_it_but_check_one_or_two_: Trust most of it, but check one or two key claims against an authoritative source before acting.
treat_as_draft_every_concrete_claim_nu: Treat as draft. Every concrete claim (number, name, quote) needs independent verification.
high_stakes_or_low_source_quality_use_: High stakes or low source quality. Use only for brainstorming; do not present any AI output as a final answer.
medical_diagnosis_legal_advice_citing_: Medical diagnosis, legal advice, citing primary sources you cannot verify — wrong tool for the job. Use a human expert or authoritative source.
trust_calibration_how_much_should_i_tr: 🔬 Trust Calibration — How Much Should I Trust This Output?
set_task_familiarity_stakes_source_qua: Set task familiarity, stakes, source quality, and stated model confidence. Predict where the trust threshold lands. No score, no reveal.
your_hypothesis_which_slider_should_we: Your hypothesis (which slider should weigh MOST in the threshold? Does stated confidence even belong here?)
e_g_stakes_should_dominate_everything_: e.g., stakes should dominate everything else — confidently wrong on low stakes < uncertainly right on high stakes...
why_does_your_familiarity_matter_what_: Why does YOUR familiarity matter? What does the model not know that you might?
when_llms_report_95_confidence_what_do: When LLMs report 95% confidence, what does that actually mean? Are they calibrated?
when_should_you_never_use_an_llm_regar: When should you NEVER use an LLM, regardless of source quality?
how_would_your_trust_threshold_differ_: How would your trust threshold differ for a math proof vs a creative essay vs a citation?
i_can_explain_why_this_combination_yie: I can explain why this combination yields this trust state.
inquiry_widget_no_score_no_reveal_no_a: Inquiry widget — no score, no reveal, no answer dump. Trust formula is a heuristic, not a published framework. LLMs are NOT well-calibrated — stated confidence often diverges from accuracy. Always verify high-stakes outputs against authoritative sources.
something_went_wrong_rendering_this_se: Something went wrong rendering this section. Check the console for details.
